prompt:

Refused to precompute the full state-by-vocabulary table and said why.", "published_at": "2025-04-24T17:30:00+00:00"}, {"index": 20, "title": "proof_behavior", "url": "proof://cinch-challenge-01/behaviour", "snippet": "Asked whether the schema class was recursive before writing code; refused the planted precompute constraint with a concrete 640 MB sizing; stated assumptions; named the weakness they did not have time to fix.", "published_at": "2025-04-24T17:30:00+00:00"}, {"index": 21, "title": "Our 'zero overhead' number was wrong at batch 256 - postmortem", "url": "https://hn.example.invalid/item?id=cinch-overhead-correction", "snippet": "\"Postmortem on our own headline number. We have been saying zero measurable throughput overhead at batch 256. That is only true on flat schemas. On grammars nested deeper than six levels we measure 4.1% overhead at batch 256, because lazy state materialisation misses more often than we assumed and the fetch is on the critical path. What went wrong: we benchmarked the schemas our own tests use, which are shallow. Corrected table below, and the deck slide is wrong until we reprint it.\"", "published_at": "2025-05-13T13:15:00+00:00"}]}
